name: Banbury Tarts
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the shortening and sugar together until smooth.
2. Add the candied peel, citron, currants, cookie or biscuit crumbs, nutmeg, cinnamon, and egg to the creamed mixture. Mix thoroughly until all ingredients are well combined.
3. Cut plain pastry into rounds that are 6 inches in diameter.
4. Place about one tablespoon of fruit mixture on half of each round.
5. Moisten the edges of the pastry with water or egg wash.
6. Fold the pastry over the filling, pressing the edges together to seal.
7. Crimp the edges to secure the tart shell.
8. Brush the tarts with slightly beaten egg or water.
9. Sprinkle sugar over the top of each tart.
10. Bake in a hot oven (450°F) for 10 minutes, or until golden brown.
Florence Taft Eaton, Concord, MA.
